finding: ILIKE term is a bound parameter concatenated with literal '%' inside SQL (sqlx compile-checked); no SQL injection.
search clause ANDed inside WHERE user_id=$1 in both SELECT and COUNT, OR-group fully parenthesized; cannot widen ownership scope.
LIKE metacharacters (%,_) in user term are not escaped (treated as wildcards). Harmless: scoped to the user's own rows; matches the mcp convention.
COUNT uses byte-identical predicate to the page SELECT; total stays consistent with the filtered page.
NULL description → description ILIKE yields NULL (falsy) inside the OR; name arm still applies. Correct, no error.
normalize_search trims + blanks→None; blank short-circuits via $N::text IS NULL returning all rows. Covered by TEST-2/TEST-5.
Both queries map errors via AppError::database_error; COUNT null-coalesces via unwrap_or(0); no new panic/unwrap introduced.
SELECT and COUNT are two non-transactional statements; a concurrent write between them could make total disagree. Pre-existing pattern, unchanged by this diff; not a regression.
search filter ANDed inside existing user_id scope in both queries; RequirePermissions<(ProjectsRead,)> gate untouched. TEST-6 confirms isolation.
Adding search to the shared PaginationQuery advertised an ignored search param on Project.listConversations (contract wart). Reviewer suggested a dedicated query type.
search: Option<String> is schemars-visible → OpenAPI marks it required:false, matching page/limit convention.
N/A: stateless backend read path; no shared/mutable server state introduced.
N/A: backend-only change; no user-facing markup or interaction surface.
SQL predicate matches mcp/repository.rs convention exactly, applied identically to SELECT and COUNT, fully parameterized.
normalize_search extracted into a named fn (vs mcp inline) — justified for Tier-1 unit testability; functionally identical. Conformant.
No test exercises filtered set spanning multiple pages: total-vs-truncated-page consistency (COUNT ignoring LIMIT) never asserted.
No test covers a term matching MULTIPLE projects, nor LIKE metacharacters in the user term.
Tests hit real HTTP + real DB, assert total and concrete names[]; negatives assert empty. No vacuous-pass risk.
Leading-wildcard ILIKE is non-sargable (seq scan) but WHERE user_id=$1 filters to a small per-user set first; matches the sanctioned mcp pattern. Acceptable.
Doc-comment on the search field is clear/accurate, flows to OpenAPI via schemars; no user-facing runtime strings. N/A.
ProjectListQuery::resolved() clamps byte-identically to PaginationQuery::resolved (page[1,1e6]/limit[1,100], defaults 1/20); overflow guarantee preserved.
New ProjectListQuery Deserialize faithfully mirrors the Raw-struct pattern; empty ?search= -> Some("") -> normalize_search -> None. Correct.

Checked whether removing search from PaginationQuery broke the 2nd caller (chat_extension list_project_conversations): it did not — FIX-A ADDED ProjectListQuery, PaginationQuery still has page/limit; 2nd caller only uses resolved(). No regression.
ProjectListQuery JsonSchema Option fields -> search surfaces on Project.list OpenAPI only; conversations endpoint keeps shared PaginationQuery. FIX-A achieves its goal.
TEST-8 non-vacuous: 3 matches + 1 decoy, limit=2 -> total==3 (COUNT ignores LIMIT) AND page len==2.
TEST-9 non-vacuous + deterministic: 'Design notes' decoy excluded, names sorted before compare, '%25' drives literal-percent wildcard path. No ORDER BY tie-break reliance.
ProjectListQuery field/clamp/Deserialize duplication is intentional per the per-endpoint-query convention (mcp ListAccessibleServersQuery); acceptable, not a maintainability defect.
TEST-6 confirms filter stays ownership-scoped; cross-user match returns total 0. No authz widening.
Both queries propagate DB errors via map_err(AppError::database_error); COUNT unwrap_or(0) unchanged. No new swallow.
Non-sargable leading-wildcard ILIKE, but user_id-scoped first; matches accepted mcp convention. Not a new defect.
